name: Onyx Coffee Lab
notes: 2026 #1 World's Best. James Beard Outstanding Bar finalist. Solar powered roastery. 8 AR locations.

name: Tim Wendelboe
notes: Micro-roastery + espresso bar. Light roasts, direct trade pioneer. Also a training center.

name: Alquimia Coffee
notes: Micro-roastery at origin. Run by most successful WBC coach. Promotes Salvadoran coffee.

name: Apartment Coffee
notes: Best coffee shop in Asia two years running. White minimalist studio. Single-origin focus.

name: Gota Coffee Experts
notes: Peruvian-inspired. Specialty education focus. Precision roasting.

name: La Cabra
notes: Nordic minimalism meets obsessive sourcing. Also in Aarhus.

name: Coffee Collective
notes: Direct trade cooperative model. Multiple Copenhagen locations.

name: The Barn
notes: Never blends beans. Full traceability. Leading European roaster since 2010.

name: Bonanza Coffee
notes: Berlin third-wave stalwart.

name: Five Elephant
notes: Coffee + cake destination. Also had a location in Chengdu, China.

name: Koffee Mameya Kakeru
notes: Coffee sommelier concept. Curated tasting experiences. Reservation recommended for dine-in.

name: Fuglen
notes: Norwegian-Japanese crossover. Vintage furniture. Sangubashi location in restored Japanese house. Coffee tasting course by reservation.

name: Onibus Coffee
notes: Pioneered light/fruit-forward roasts in Japan. Original Nakameguro location is the crown jewel. Multiple Tokyo locations.

name: Leaves Coffee Roasters
notes: Founded by former boxing champion Yasuo. Aims to become world roasting champion. Light roasts.

name: Bear Pond Espresso
notes: Founded by Katsu Tanaka (ex-NYC). No photos rule. Don't linger. Tiny, serious. Flower Child blend is the signature.

name: Ult Coffee
notes: Precision brewing. #24 in World's 100 Best 2026.

name: Sweet Bloom Coffee Roasters
notes: National-level light roast quality. Won basically every award. Worth the drive from Denver. Also in Arvada.

name: Huckleberry Roasters
notes: Sunnyside location has floor-to-ceiling windows into roastery. Multiple Denver locations. Heavy hitter in local scene.

name: Corvus Coffee Roasters
notes: Direct trade, sustainability focus. Houses Fox & the Raven bakery. Exotic/Reserve Geshas sell out in advance. 5 locations.

name: Boxcar Coffee Roasters
notes: Boulder-born. RiNo location is a neighborhood favorite. Single-origin focused.

name: Crema Coffee House
notes: Widely credited with starting Denver's third-wave movement. Larimer St. Also has bodega in Denver Central Market.

name: Middle State Coffee
notes: Trained at Ninety Plus Coffee. Seasonal single-origins unavailable elsewhere in Denver. Natural wines. Best of 2026 Award.

name: Novo Coffee
notes: OG Denver roaster since 2002. 4 shops. Friday cupping and roastery tour ($30). Artisan coffee pioneer.

name: APF. Kafe
notes: Slayer + Proud Mary beans. Residential Tianhe area. Family-friendly. Stunning white interior.

name: Buzz Coffee
notes: Possibly best coffee in Guangzhou per Bean Portal. Two espresso options + daily aeropress. Light roasted anaerobic geisha.

name: Rose Coffee Garden
notes: 10+ years. NO MENU — only coffee. Custom-built grinder. Enthusiast gathering place. Buy beans, drink free. Zhongshan Road alley.

name: FrontStreet Coffee
notes: Long-established home-roasting shop. Largest specialty bean selection in GZ — Geisha, Blue Mountain at accessible prices. Yandun Road.

name: Spring Hill Coffee
notes: Choose your bean type. Freshly roasted on-site. Good for remote work. Two locations.

name: HAY Coffee
notes: Understated, stylish. Near APF Kafe in the Tianhe specialty corridor. Floor-to-ceiling glass.

name: Yuyuan Coffee
notes: Pioneering boutique shop since 2005. Taiwanese owner Jeff. One of Guangzhou's first specialty cafes.

name: Song
notes: Michelin-starred coffee experience. Futuristic design. Cheaper than Costa UK. Excellent cakes. Taikoo Hui Mall.

name: 3fe
notes: Colin Harmon — three-time Irish Barista Champion. Author of 'What I Know About Running Coffee Shops.'

name: Coffeewerk + Press
notes: Multi-roaster (30+ worldwide). Mini art gallery. Coffee merchandise. Specialty since 2015.

name: Espresso Alchemy
notes: #31 World's 100 Best 2026.

name: Project C
notes: 29 Hysan Ave near Lee Gardens. Standing-room espresso bar (~3-4 spots). Rotating beans every 2-3 weeks — exposes customers to different roasters/origins. Co-founder Kenneth Lam. Hidden in plain sight on Hysan Ave.

name: The Cupping Room
notes: Hong Kong third-wave pioneer. Multiple locations (Sheung Wan, Wan Chai, Central, etc.). Founder is a former HK Barista Champion. In-house roastery.

name: The Coffee Academics
notes: Pioneer of third-wave coffee in HK since launch a decade+ ago. Sources only top 5% Arabica from 50+ independent farms worldwide. Multiple HK locations plus international expansion.

name: % Arabica
notes: Global brand originally from Kyoto, JP. Multiple HK locations (Star Ferry pier, IFC Mall, Causeway Bay). Light roasts, signature Kyoto Geisha blend. Small focused menu — espresso, latte, pour-over.

name: Halfway Coffee
notes: Sheung Wan community favorite. Vintage interior, served in vintage Chinese-style porcelain cups. Single-origin focus.

name: 18 Grams
notes: HK third-wave pioneer founded 2007. Multiple HK locations. One of the cafes that started the third-wave movement in the city.

name: Sensory Zero
notes: Third-wave pioneer. Multiple HK locations. Single-origin program with seasonal rotation.

name: OMA Specialty Coffee
notes: Tai Hang neighborhood specialty shop. Featured on the Grounds Club HK guide. Signal data limited — needs in-person verification.

name: Urban Coffee Roaster
notes: TST shop on Kimberley St. In-house roastery. Third-wave pioneer in Kowloon. Multiple HK locations.

name: Prufrock Coffee
notes: 23-25 Leather Lane. Founded by Gwilym Davies — UK Barista Champion & World Barista Champion 2009. One of London's original third-wave anchors. Hosts La Marzocco machines.

name: WatchHouse Bermondsey
notes: 199 Bermondsey St — original flagship inside a restored 19th-century watchhouse. In-house roastery. Multi-location: Seven Dials, St. Mary Axe, Spitalfields, Marylebone, etc.

name: Catalyst
notes: 51 Aybrook St, Marylebone. Multi-roaster. Frequent UK Barista of the Year venue. Small focused menu, single-origin espresso program with seasonal rotation.

name: Allpress Espresso Bar
notes: 58 Redchurch St, Shoreditch. New Zealand-origin roaster (Auckland 1989). Pioneer of NZ-style espresso in London. In-house Roastery Cafe on Dalston Lane. Multiple London locations.

name: Caravan Coffee Roasters
notes: 11-13 Exmouth Market — original flagship. In-house roastery (now at Bankside). Brunch + coffee crossover. Multiple London locations including King's Cross, Fitzrovia, Bankside.

name: Climpson & Sons
notes: 67 Broadway Market — iconic East London cafe + Saturday market stall. In-house roastery (The Arch). Iconic E5/E8 specialty anchor since 2005.

name: Origin Coffee Shoreditch
notes: Cornwall-based roaster (founded Helston, 2004). Multiple London cafes including Shoreditch, Southwark, British Library. Direct-trade pioneer, single-origin focused.

name: Rosslyn Coffee
district: City of London / Queen Victoria St
notes: 78 Queen Victoria St — high-volume espresso bar engineered for City foot traffic without sacrificing quality. Also Leadenhall Market. Featured by European Coffee Trip for ops excellence.

name: Kaffeine
notes: 66 Great Titchfield St — Australian-owned, founded by Peter Dore-Smith. Multi-roaster: features Square Mile + rotating guest roasters. Eastcastle St second location.

name: Esters
notes: 55 Kynaston Rd — owner-operated neighborhood specialty. Tight focused menu. Much-loved N16 anchor.

name: Saint Espresso
notes: In-house roaster with locations across Camden, Angel, Soho. Single-origin program with seasonal rotation.

name: Artisan Coffee
notes: 372 King St, W6 0RX — closest station Stamford Brook. Hidden King Street gem. Specialty coffee + cakes/pastries. Retail beans available. 4.6/5 across 1,180+ reviews.

name: Workshop Coffee Holborn
notes: 27 Clerkenwell Rd / Holborn area — historic flagship. Workshop is a foundational UK specialty roaster, in-house roasted. Single-origin obsessed. Founded 2011.

name: Workshop Coffee Marylebone
notes: Wigmore St / Barrett St area. Central Marylebone location of the Workshop Coffee brand. Single-origin espresso program.

name: Workshop Coffee Belgravia (Cafe & Academy)
notes: 22 Eccleston St, SW1W 9LT — newest flagship cafe + training academy, opened February 2026. Hands-on barista learning space combined with full Workshop espresso/filter program.

name: Companion Coffee
notes: Weinmeisterstraße. Tea + coffee crossover concept. Curated multi-roaster with rotating guest beans. Quiet, design-forward Mitte space.

name: Father Carpenter
notes: Münzstraße 21, hidden inside a Mitte courtyard. Brunch + coffee anchor. Curated multi-roaster — frequently pours The Barn and rotating European roasters.

name: Refinery
notes: Third-wave specialty roaster + cafe. Boppstraße area. Single-origin espresso program.

name: Coffee Circle Torstraße
notes: Torstraße flagship cafe. Coffee Circle is a major Berlin roaster (founded 2010) with a direct-trade program in Ethiopia and Africa. Multiple Berlin locations (Bergmann, Rosa-Luxemburg, Maybachufer, Wedding).

name: Westberlin
notes: Friedrichstraße. Iconic third-wave anchor since ~2010. Magazine + coffee concept. Curated multi-roaster pouring The Barn and rotating European roasters.

name: Goldhahn & Sampson
notes: High-end specialty + culinary bookshop concept. Curated multi-roaster pouring some of Europe's best (Tim Wendelboe, La Cabra, Drop Coffee, etc.). Wilmersdorfer Str (Charlottenburg) and Dunckerstr (Prenzlauer Berg) locations.

name: Distrikt Coffee
notes: Bergstraße 68 (Mitte). Australian-style brunch + serious coffee. Curated multi-roaster — rotates through Berlin's best (The Barn, Bonanza) and international guests.

name: 19grams Schlesi
notes: Skalitzer Str — Schlesisches Tor location. 19grams is a Berlin third-wave pioneer (in-house roastery). Multiple locations: Alex, Chausseestr, Tres Cabezas Café.

name: Concierge Coffee
notes: Paul-Lincke-Ufer (Kreuzberg). Small specialty bar with tight focused menu. Single-origin espresso program.

name: Kaffee 9
notes: Hermannstraße. Well-regarded community specialty multi-roaster. Neukölln anchor.

name: Röststätte Berlin
notes: Ackerstraße. In-house roastery + cafe. Espresso + filter + pour-over program. Visible roaster.

name: Isla Coffee Berlin
notes: Hermannstraße. Specialty + zero-waste ethos — closed-loop sustainability program. In-house roaster, single-origin focus.

name: La Cabra East Village
notes: 152 Second Ave. NYC outpost of Copenhagen world-class roaster La Cabra. Cardamom buns + Nordic-style coffee program.

name: La Cabra Roastery Bushwick
notes: 1329 Willoughby Avenue — La Cabra's North American roasting hub. Sleek oak structures, library-style carrels, visible roasting setup. Espresso + filter + pour-over with full Nordic precision.

name: Devoción Flatiron
notes: 25 E 20th St. Devoción flies Colombian beans direct from farm to NY within 10 days of harvest. Their model defines farm-to-cup transparency in NYC.

name: Devoción Williamsburg
notes: 69 Grand St — NYC roastery + flagship cafe. Living plant wall, visible Loring roaster. Colombian farm-direct sourcing model.

name: Sey Coffee
notes: 18 Grattan St. Founders Tobin Polk and Lance Schnorenberg trained with Joanna Alm of Drop Coffee (Stockholm). Nordic-style washed-only coffees, brewed with Aeropress instead of typical pour-over. Glass-walled roastery. Gratuity-free model. SEY = 'YES' backwards.

name: Café Integral
notes: 149 Elizabeth St. Nicaraguan-coffee focused. Founded by César Vega — direct relationships with Nicaraguan farms.

name: Café Leon Dore
notes: 214 Mulberry St. Coffee bar inside the Aimé Leon Dore flagship. Greek-inspired space and menu (Greek frappes, freddo cappuccinos). Design-led but with a serious specialty program.

name: Café Lyria
notes: Noho specialty cafe. Tight focused menu.

name: Black Fox Coffee Financial District
notes: 70 Pine St — flagship Financial District location. Black Fox roasts in-house and runs high-volume specialty across FiDi, Midtown East, and Hudson Yards.

name: Coffee Project NY East Village
notes: 239 E 5th St. Co-founder Chi Sum Ngai is a multi-time US Brewers Cup champion. Multi-location: East Village, Chelsea, Fort Greene, Long Island City. Education-forward — coffee classes and brew labs.

name: Loveless Coffees
notes: Bushwick. Founders Will Douglas and Joe Wieczorek (formerly Spectrum Coffees in Red Hook). Loring Falcon roaster. 'NY-Nordic' style — Norwegian-precision medium-light roasts with NYC character. Transparency: purchase prices publicly listed. Partnered with April Coffee for retail.

name: Dayglow Williamsburg
notes: LA-origin multi-roaster + subscription service. ~10 different pour-over options at any time. Coffee mocktails. Transforms into 'Niteglow' (beer/cocktails) in the evening. Features April, La Cabra, Coffee Collective. Also roasts own coffee.

name: Principles GI Coffee House
notes: 9th St area, between Park Slope and Gowanus. Founded by Katie Bishop (former Marine and bassoonist) — inspired by 1960s antiwar GI coffeehouses. Curated multi-roaster featuring Passenger Coffee (Lancaster, PA) and others. Community-and-conversation focus.

name: Roasting Plant Lower East Side
notes: Lower East Side flagship. Atypical specialty model — Javabot vacuum-tube system roasts and grinds beans on demand per drink. Unusual but quality is taken seriously; single-origin and farm-direct sourcing.

name: Variety Coffee Roasters
notes: 368 Graham Ave — Williamsburg flagship. Variety roasts in-house. Multi-location across Brooklyn and Manhattan (Chelsea, Forsyth, etc.). Solid third-wave anchor.

name: Stumptown Coffee Ace Hotel
notes: 18 W 29th St — Ace Hotel NYC flagship. Stumptown's NYC anchor. PDX-origin roaster (Hair Bender espresso). Pioneers of US third-wave.

name: Felix Roasting Co.
notes: 450 Park Ave South. Ornate Italianate-meets-modern interior. Semi-luxe positioning but real specialty program — in-house roasting, single-origin focus, creative signature drinks (orange-blossom latte, etc.).

name: Houndstooth Coffee
notes: Austin-origin (founded 2010) with strong DFW presence. Multi-roaster — features Tweed (sister brand) and other Texas specialty roasters. Multiple Dallas locations.

name: Ascension Coffee
notes: Longest-running DFW specialty chain. In-house roaster. Design District flagship + multiple locations. Coffee + food crossover with serious espresso program.

name: Cultivar Coffee
notes: Roaster + cafes. Well-known Dallas third-wave anchor. Lower Greenville flagship plus Oak Cliff. Single-origin focused.

name: Noble Coyote Coffee Roasters
notes: 4642 Bryan St, Expo Park. Roaster + cafe with direct-trade focus. Long-running Dallas specialty roaster.

name: Oak Cliff Coffee Roasters
notes: Bishop Arts area. One of the OGs of Dallas specialty coffee. Long-running local roaster.

name: Tweed Coffee
notes: Newer Dallas specialty roaster. Sister brand to Houndstooth — strong third-wave craft, single-origin focused. Served at Houndstooth locations.

name: Pax & Beneficia
notes: In-house roasting + Mediterranean/Middle Eastern food crossover. Multi-location DFW. Serious espresso program despite the food-forward concept.

name: Murray Street Coffee Shop
notes: 103 Murray St — Deep Ellum institution. Multi-roaster specialty cafe.

name: Opening Bell Coffee
notes: Cedars neighborhood. Long-running specialty multi-roaster + live music venue.

name: George Coffee & Provisions
notes: Carrollton specialty cafe. Modern multi-roaster with strong filter program.

name: Weekend Coffee
notes: Newer Bishop Arts specialty cafe. Strong design aesthetic. Curated multi-roaster.

name: Magnolias Sous Le Pont
notes: Bishop Arts cafe + bakery + bookstore concept. French-inspired. Specialty coffee program.

name: Mokah Coffee & Tea
notes: 2826 Elm St, Deep Ellum. Established specialty cafe + tea program.

name: Hola Café
notes: Latin American-inspired Bishop Arts specialty cafe. Curated multi-roaster.

name: LDU Coffee
notes: Australian-style specialty cafe. Strong flat-white culture. Multi-roaster.

name: Café Duro
notes: Knox-Henderson area. Newer design-forward specialty cafe. Curated multi-roaster.

name: Peaberry Coffee
notes: Oak Cliff specialty cafe. Curated multi-roaster.

name: Method Coffee Bar
notes: Legacy West area, Plano. North DFW suburb specialty anchor — strong filter program. Often cited by r/Dallas as the best north-of-635 specialty.
